message:
feat(fcp-protocol/session): MINIMUM_SUITE floor + suite_rank ordering (br-crkft.4)

Adds an explicit floor to suite negotiation so a misconfigured peer
or a stale responder preference list cannot negotiate below a
known-acceptable suite.

- `MINIMUM_SUITE: SessionCryptoSuite` — public constant, currently
  Suite1. Bumping is a deployment-breaking change per
  docs/protocol/session-handshake.md (added in crkft.5).
- `suite_rank` — private `const fn` that encodes the strength
  ordering. Suite1=1, Suite2=2. Future suite variants must assign a
  rank; this is the ONE site that knows "Suite2 is stronger than
  Suite1."
- `negotiate_suite` now filters by `suite_rank(*suite) >= floor` so
  any suite weaker than `MINIMUM_SUITE` cannot be selected.

Rank semantics matter: the rank is a STRENGTH ordering, not a
PREFERENCE ordering. Responder preference order still determines
which mutual above-floor suite is picked.

Tests:
- negotiate_suite_accepts_at_or_above_floor: happy path at the floor
- minimum_suite_equals_current_weakest: mechanical checkpoint that
  catches a floor update forgotten during suite-deprecation
- suite_rank_is_monotonic: trust the ordering

13/13 session tests green including crkft.2/.3 regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
